title: ‘It feels like home’: Chinatown celebrates the Year of the Horse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chinatown's annual Lunar New Year parade celebrated the Year of the Horse with about 10 lion dance troupes performing.
The celebration, organized by Chinatown Main Street and the Chinese Consolidated Benevolent Association, included traditional lion dances.
Gund Kwok, an all-women dance troupe, performed with a horse costume, highlighting creativity and challenging traditional gender roles.

title: Boston 25 News promotes Shiri Spear to chief meteorologist
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Shiri Spear has been promoted to chief meteorologist at Boston 25 News, succeeding Kevin Lemanowicz.
Spear will oversee the station's weather coverage, lead the meteorology team, and participate in community outreach.
Spear holds a master's in atmospheric science and is a certified broadcast meteorologist by the American Meteorological Society.

title: R.I. prosecutor who told police ‘I’m an AG’ during arrest is back at work
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Rhode Island prosecutor Devon Flanagan returned to work after unpaid leave for misdemeanor trespassing in Newport.
Flanagan was reassigned from the Appellate Unit to Providence County Superior Court's daily calendar.
Police bodycam footage showed Flanagan invoking her prosecutor title and challenging officers during her arrest.

title: Should pajamas be banned at Logan Airport? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 265 Boston.com readers polled on banning pajamas from Logan Airport; 48% for, 42% against, 11% say it depends.
Some readers argue pajamas in public are disrespectful and call for a return to more formal travel attire.
Other readers emphasize comfort over formality in airports, citing stress of flying and personal choice in dress.

title: ‘A crisis of Trump’s creation’: Mass. politicians respond to strikes on Iran
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts politicians condemned Trump's strikes on Iran, labeling them as illegal and calling for congressional votes on war powers resolutions.
Sen. Elizabeth Warren and Sen. Ed Markey criticized the military actions, emphasizing the importance of diplomacy over escalation in the Middle East.
Reps. Seth Moulton and Katherine Clark highlighted domestic issues, arguing military focus detracts from pressing economic and social concerns at home.

title: Carmelo Anthony, Tracy McGrady impressed with Celtics after win vs. 76ers
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Carmelo Anthony and Tracy McGrady praised the Celtics for their strong performance in a 114-98 win over the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown and Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 27 points each, showcasing effective team strategy and adjustments.
The Celtics face the Milwaukee Bucks next, continuing their season with a game scheduled for 7:30 p.m. tonight.

title: Fatal fire under investigation in Roxbury
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: One person was found dead in a residential building fire at 4 Ellingwood Street in Roxbury on February 27.
Boston Police and Fire Departments are investigating the fire, which occurred around 2:55 p.m. on Friday.
The fire was active on all three floors of the building, according to Deputy Chief Gerard Viola.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in March
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Bambola, a new Italian supper club with Roman and Southern cuisine, opens late March at 225 Northern Ave, Seaport.
Common Craft South Boston, led by James Beard winner Tony Messina, opens mid-March at 85 Damrell St.
MEZŌ Mediterranean, offering Greek dishes and meze, opens March 4 at 551 High St., Dedham.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about Tuesday’s ‘wintry mix’
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston meteorologists predict a wintry mix of snow, sleet, and rain affecting travel on Tuesday afternoon and evening.
Cindy Fitzgibbon forecasts light snow transitioning to rain with slick conditions during the Tuesday evening commute.
A total lunar eclipse will occur early Tuesday morning, visible briefly as the moon sets on the horizon.

title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta excelled with 27 points and 17 rebounds in the Celtics' 114-98 victory over the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown recorded 27 points, eight rebounds, and eight assists, showcasing exceptional passing against the 76ers.
Baylor Scheierman played despite a thumb injury, contributing both defensively and with a well-timed three-pointer.
